A_methods:
Associate Director, thanking us for the kind offer and assuring us that we would
be called upon for our advice and counsel in the near future. In August I
visited with Dr. Mobley, Dr. Spanton, and the new Acting Head of the Vocational
Education Division, Dr. Arnold. In September I was invited to represent our
association at the Rural Education Conference in Pittsburgh. This group is a
division of the NEA.
Dr. Swanson of the University of Minnesota, at my request, bas prepared
a draft of recommended operating policies for our organization which will be
A_results:
presented at the meeting in Kansas City for consideration by the association.
Dr. G. E. Timmons of Michigan State University served as chairman of a
committee for giving recognition to outstanding teacher trainers in agricultural
education each year. The proposed plan will be presented at the meeting in
Kansas City.
During the year our association has also formally affiliated with the
Rural Education Branch of the NEA and with the Associated Organizations for
Teacher Education. This action was approved at our Los Angeles meeting. I
have asked Dr. Milo J. Peterson to be our official representative to the
ACTE meetings. He is currently serving on the Advisory Council of this organization. He will also attend the meeting of the Land-Grant College Association in November and will represent our association if the need arises.
Dr. G. F. Ekstrom of the University of Missouri has prepared an interesting
program for the meeting of the teacher trainers in agricultural education during
the AVA Convention, and the breakfast meeting which will take place on Wednesday
morning, December 6, I hope as many teacher trainers as possible can attend
and I shall be looking forward to renewing old acquaintances at that time.
David R. McClay
